result.prompt:
Use image 1 and image 2 only as the absolute source of pose-and-structure truth. Use image 3 only as a minimal face-and-hair identity reference. Do not use image 3 for pose, hand action, silhouette direction, robe motion, spellcasting, held items, or final rendering finish. This pass is a pose-lock pass, not a final design pass.

Keep only these source facts from the original image and preserve them exactly: exact body-facing direction, exact body lean, exact torso tilt, exact head angle, exact eye direction, exact shoulder line, exact hand placement, exact pelvis position, exact leg spacing, exact knee height, exact calf width, exact foot placement, and exact overall body placement inside the frame. Preserve the full visible body from hair to shoes. Keep the exact composition, empty padding, and center alignment of the original source frame. Do not redesign the anatomy, the pose, or the character placement inside the frame.

Never mirror the pose. Never flip the facing direction. Never turn the original right-facing idle into a left-facing or front-facing pose. Do not make the stance symmetrical. Do not make the shoulders square to the camera. Keep the same rightward lean and right-facing idle read as the original source keyframe.

This is a neutral idle sprite keyframe. Both hands must remain empty. Do not show fire, magic flame, spell orb, book, card, scroll, wand, or any active combat effect. Do not convert the idle into a casting pose. Keep the original idle hand pose exactly.

Design a compact 3.5-head SD fantasy academy mage sprite. This is only a pose-lock pass, so prioritize body direction, idle posture, and silhouette stability over decorative detail. He should still read as a handsome young elite mage with bright blond hair, ruby-red eyes, and a blue-black academy robe, but the pose contract is more important than detailed styling in this pass.

Face and hair: keep a youthful handsome elite mage read with light blond hair and ruby-red irises. Use a neat half-swept academy hairstyle, not messy hair. Keep the face sharp, clean, and disciplined rather than cute-chibi, but do not spend sprite budget on excessive facial detail in this pass.

Outfit silhouette: this is a robe, not a cape, not a coat, and not a tunic. Use a long blue-black academy robe silhouette that hangs straight downward with weight. The robe must not flare backward, trail like a cape, behave like a scarf, or flutter in idle. Keep the silhouette simple and stable. No belt, no waist sash, no wrist guard, no gloves. Show only a narrow chest opening with a small glimpse of white shirt and red tie if visible.

Rendering style for in-game asset: high-resolution dot graphic character sprite, polished 2D game sprite, online action RPG style, Korean fantasy RPG aesthetic, pixel-based rendering with refined detail, smooth silhouette, clean anatomy simplification, controlled cel shading, subtle pixel texture, transparent background. This pass should be simple, clean, readable, and structurally stable. Generate this pass as a real 1024x1024 working image that remains compatible with later 128x128 runtime reduction.

Important: preserve the original keyframe composition exactly, preserve the original right-facing idle body direction, keep both hands empty, and do not redesign the anatomy or pose.
